creator: cyberbotx@cyberbotx.com
text: cups-filters doesn't have a direct dependency to graphics/jpeg. It has a jpeg dependency when the IMAGEFILTERS option (which is on by default) it used, but it uses the JPEG library selected by the ports system, which is determined by the logic of /usr/ports/Mk/Uses/jpeg.mk, which I do NOT maintain. I will continue to stress that this was not a cups-filters problem, it was a problem with the configuration of your system, as your latest post demonstrated. I am glad you resolved it, but please do not throw the blame at cups-filters when it is not the problem.
